title: Ensure that the etcd pod specification file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the etcd pod specification file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The etcd pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml controls various parameters that set the behavior of the etcd service in the master node. etcd is a highly- available key-value store which Kubernetes uses for persistent storage of all of its REST API object. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml Verify that the permissions are 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chmod 644 /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml Default Value: By default, /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml file has permissions of 640. References: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- This control requires verification of the file permissions of /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml on the host filesystem of the control plane node.
- Kubernetes APIs and kubectl do not expose host OS file permissions for static pod manifest files.
- The audit and remediation steps both require direct OS-level access (e.g., stat, chmod), which cannot be performed via Kubernetes API or kubectl.
- Therefore, this check cannot be automated via Kubernetes-native APIs or kubectl and requires manual verification.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the Kubernetes control plane node where the etcd static pod is configured.
2. Run the following command to check the permissions of the etcd manifest file:
   ```
   stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml
   ```
3. Verify that the output is 644 or a more restrictive value (e.g., 640, 600).
4. If the permissions are more permissive than 644 (e.g., 666, 777), remediate by running:
   ```
   chmod 644 /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml
   ```
5. Confirm compliance by re-running the stat command and ensuring the permissions are 644 or more restrictive.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
